slides.title: Giving your time may *add years* to it.
slides.sub: Regular volunteering is linked to lower inflammation and a longer life. The reason is built into your biology.

slides.title: Helping is a health habit
slides.body: Showing up for others on a regular basis is one of the *quietly powerful* levers for living longer and feeling well.

slides.title: Prosocial reward kicks in
slides.body: Helping someone activates the brain's *reward and oxytocin pathways*. The warm feeling after giving is real chemistry, not just sentiment.

slides.title: Lower stress, lower inflammation
slides.body: Those same pathways can dial down stress signaling. Over time that is linked to *lower chronic inflammation*, a key driver of aging.

slides.title: Linked to a longer life
slides.body: In large studies, regular volunteers show *lower all-cause mortality*. Connection and purpose appear to protect the body, not just the mood.

slides.title: Make it regular, keep it small
slides.body: Consistency matters more than hours. A *steady monthly shift* at a food bank or shelter can support both purpose and health.

slides.title: Turn good habits into *good years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the sleep, food, movement and steps behind a long life, and turns them into one daily Health Score you can actually follow.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Volunteering does more than feel good. Regular giving is linked to lower inflammation and a longer life. 🌿🤝

The mechanism is real: helping activates reward and oxytocin pathways, which can lower stress and chronic inflammation over time. Keep it small and steady, and save this for your next free weekend. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the habits behind a longer life into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Volunteering May Add Years to Your Life
